subject: Chemistry
solution: Cumene (isopropylbenzene) is oxidised in the presence of air to cumene hydroperoxide. It is converted to phenol and acetone by treating it with dilute acid.

subject: Chemistry
options: A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
solution:
For Assertion: The acetals are unstable in the presence of aqueous solutions of acid because they undergo hydrolysis in the presence of acidic conditions to produce the parent aldehyde or ketone or alcohols. But are stable and do not undergo hydrolysis in the presence of a base or under neutral conditions.

Hence assertion is correct.

For reason: Alkoxide ion (RO–) is not considered a good leaving group

Hence reason must be false.

So, the correct option is A

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In aqueous medium basic strength is dependent on electron density on nitrogen as well as solvation of cation formed after accepting H+. In protic solvent like water, the order of basic strength is

Me2NH>MeNH2>Me3N>NH3.

This happens beacuse in case of Me2NH there will be optimum (+I) and solvation while in MeNH2 has more significant hydration than Me3N.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Step 1:

(∵NO2​  is m-directing group while CH3 group is​  o− and p− directing group. Thus, Br group attach at o− position with respect to CH3 and m-position with respect to NO2 group)

Step 2:

On reduction with Sn/HCl, NO2-  group changes to  −NH2​ group.

Step 3:

 On treatment with NaNO2+HCl; NH2 changes to diazo group.

Step 4:

On treatment with H3PO2​, only diazo group (N2Cl) reacts and gets removed.

Step 5:

On oxidation with KMnO4 ​  /OH−, −CH3​ group changes to −COOH group.

solution:
Overall reaction of metabolism of glucose in the body:
C6H12O6( s)+6O2→6CO2( g)+6H2O(l)

Extra energy used to convert  H2O(l) into H2O(g), energy left

=18002=900 kJ

⇒900=nH2O×45

nH2O=90045=20 mole 

WH2O=20×18=360 g

subject: Chemistry
options: An antibiotic must be a product of metabolism.
An antibiotic is a synthetic substance produced as a structural analogue of naturally occurring antibiotic.
An antibiotic should promote the growth or survival of microorganisms.
An antibiotic should be effective in low concentrations.
solution: An antibiotic should not promote growth or survival of microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, algae because antibiotics are chemicals that kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria and are used to treat bacterial infections. They are produced in nature by soil bacteria and fungi.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The substrate concentration influences enzyme activity. As the substrate concentration increases the rate of reaction also increases. This is because the more substrate molecules will interact with enzyme molecules, the more products will be formed.

However, after a certain concentration, further increase in substrate concentration will have no effect on the rate of reaction, since the substrate concentration will no longer be the limiting factor. At this stage, enzyme molecules become saturated and work at their maximum possible rate.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ni(CN)42-:Ni+2=

It is diamagnetic as  CN- is a strong field ligand and forces electron pairing.

Ni(CO)4:Ni=

It is diamagnetic as carbonyl group is a strong field ligand and forces electron pairing.

It is paramagnetic as chloride group is a weak field ligand.

It is diamagnetic as –CN is a  strong field ligand which causes pairing of electrons.

It is paramagnetic, here CN- is a strong field ligand

FeH2O62+:Fe2+:

It is paramagnetic, here H2O , here a weak field ligand

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cell reaction which occurs:
12H2( g)+Fe3+ (aq.) ⟶H+(aq)+Fe2+ (aq.) 
Using Nernst' equation:

E=Eo-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒0.712=(0.771-0)-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒logFe2+Fe3+=(0.771-0712)0.059=1

⇒Fe2+Fe3+=10

subject: Chemistry
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. Two of the pollutants that are emitted are hydrocarbons (unburnt fuels) and nitric oxide (NO). When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide NO2. This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and a free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g) →hν NO (g) + O(g)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g) + O2 (g) ⇌ O3 (g)

Hence, X = O, Y = NO, A = O2, B = O3

subject: Chemistry
solution: Staggered conformation has the lowest van der Waal and torsional strain. Hydrogen atoms are equally placed and there is minimum repulsion between them. Hence, it must be most stable.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The following changes occur during smelting.

(a) Most of the ferrous sulphide gets oxidised to ferrous oxide which combines with silica (flux) to form fusible slag.

2FeS + 3O2 → 2FeO + 2SO2

FeO + SiO2 → FeSiO3 (Ferrous silicate)

The slag being lighter floats and forms the upper layer. It is removed through the slag hole from time to time.

(b) During roasting or in the blast furnace if any oxide of copper is formed, it combines with FeS and is changed back into its sulphide.

2Cu2S + 3O2 → 2Cu2O + 2SO2

Cu2O + FeS → Cu2S + FeO

Ferrous oxide thus formed again combines with silica to form more slag.

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3

So, no formation of calcium silicate CaSiO3 in extraction of Cu.

Aromatic nucleophilic substitution reaction takes place via the formation of a carbanion intermediate. Hence, the rate of reaction depends on the stability of the carbanion.

Electron withdrawing effect can stabilise the carbanion. The Electron withdrawing nature of the nitro group will be minimum at the meta position.

 

Hence the correct answer is

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Phosphorus trichloride is obtained by the action of thionyl chloride with white phosphorus

P4+8SOCl2→4PCl3[A]+4SO2+2 S2Cl2

Phosphorus trichloride is a colourless oily liquid and hydrolyses in the presence of moisture give dibasic phosphorus acid.

PCl3+3H2O→H3PO3[B]+3HCl

subject: Chemistry
solution:
When calcium hydroxide is reacted with crystals of ammonium chloride, the products formed as calcium chloride, ammonia and water.

Ca(OH)2 + NH4Cl → CaCl2 + 2NH3 + 2H2O

When ammonia is reacted with water and excess carbon dioxide, it forms ammonium hydrogen carbonate which on passing through with saturated NaCl solution forms hydrogen carbonate.

NH3 + H2O + CO2 → NH4HCO3

NH4HCO3 + NaCl → NaHCO3 ↓ + NH4Cl

So, the compound A, B, and C are Ca(OH)2, NH3, NH4HCO3 respectively.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
All the given elements belong to the group - 18. All noble gases have general electronic configuration ns2np6 except helium which has 1s2. Since noble gases have stable electronic configuration, they have no tendency to accept the electron and therefore, have large positive values of electron gain enthalpy.

Values of electron gain enthalpy of all the elements are given in the below table

Element ΔegH[KJ/mol]
He +48
Ne +116
Kr +96
Xe +77

So, the order is He < Xe < Kr < Ne.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The colours of the flame are produced because of the movement of the electrons in the metal ions present in their salts. Calcium exhibits a brick-red colour. Strontium exhibits crimson colour. And the barium exhibits an apple green colour. 

K is an alkali metal. Potassium is colourless or white but when it is heated, it produces a violet flame due to the excitation of the electrons to the higher energy orbitals. Potassium burns with a light purple-coloured flame.

So, the correct option is C

a(α)+b(0)=0⇒α=0

And, α2-2α=0⇒α=0,2

For point B(1,β)

a+bβ=0

Or, β=-ba

And, 1+β2-2=0

∵a≠b therefore,

Only possibility α=0, β=1

Points are A0,0 and B1,1

x-121=y-121=-212+12+212+12

⇒x-121=y-121=-3

Equation of image circle

x+522+y+522=122

⇒x2+y2+5x+5y+12=0

fx=∫02ex-tdt

Now For x≤0

fx=∫02et-xdt=e-xe2-1

And for 0<x<2

For x≥2

fx=∫02ex-tdt=ex-2e2-1

Now for x≤0, fx is decreasing as e-x is decreasing function and x≥2, fx is increasing as ex-2 is increasing function,

So, minimum value of fx lies in x∈0,2

Applying A.M ≥ G.M in ex+e2-xwe get,

ex+e2-x2≥ex×e2-x

⇒ex+e2-x≥2e

Hence, the minimum value of fx is 2e-2=2e-1

Now multiplying and dividing by 1-x both side we get,

Now differentiating equation 1 on both sides with respect to x we get,

Now at x=-1

⇒y'(-1)=16

Now differentiating equation 2 with respect to x on both sides we get,

Now at x=-1

So, at x=-1, y'-y"=16-(-480)=496

fog ac=fog10=f1005=2007

Therefore,

fog ac+gofb=2007+32=2039

α=2

solution:
The given limit limn→∞1+2-3+4+5-6+…+(3n-2)+(3n-1)-3n2n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4 can be expressed as,limn→∞∑r=1n3r-2+3r-1-3r2n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4

=limn→∞∑r=1n3r-12n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4

=limn→∞0+3+6+9+….n terms2n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4.......(1)

We know the sum of the nth term of A.P. is given by

Sn=n22a+n-1d

Using above formula in equation(1) we get,

=limn→∞3n(n-1)22n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4

 

=limn→∞3n(n-1)22n4+4n+3-n4+5n+4

=limn→∞3(1-1n)22+4n3+3n4-1+5n3+4n4

=3(1-0)22+0+0-1+0+0

=32(2-1)=32(2+1)

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Using logical equivalence condition p→q≡~p∨q, given statement can be expressed as, 

p∧~q→p→~q

≡ ~p∧~q∨~p∨~q.......(1)

We know De Morgan's law of logical equivalence is,

~p∧q≡~p∨~q

Applying De Morgan's law in equation(1) we get,

≡ ~p∨q∨~p∨~q   ∵~~p≡p

≡ ~p∨~p∨q∨~q

≡~p∨t≡t (tautology)

Equation of the curves x=2y2 and x=1+y2

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

Sum of two integer is 66, so one number will be x and other will be 66-x,

And given M is maximum value of their product,

So let y=x66-x

⇒y=66x-x2

Now differentiating to find maxima and minima we get, 

dydx=66-2x

Now equating with zero to find point of maxima as y=66x-x2 represents a downward parabola so it will give maxima,

So dydx=0⇒66-2x=0⇒x=33,

Hence, the value of M=33×33=1089

Now solving x66-x≥5M9

⇒x66-x≥5×10899

⇒x66-x≥605

⇒x2-66x+605≤0

⇒x-11x-55≤0

So x∈11,55→total 45 numbers,

Now for probability of A, favourable outcomes will be x=3k⇒x=12,15,18,.....54→ total 15 numbers,

So probability will be 1545=13

D≥0

So, maximum value of β is 25.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
The equation of line passing through the point -3,2,3 and parallel to a line with direction ratios 3,3,-1 will be,

x+33=y-23=z-3-1=λ

Now let any point on the line will be, M3λ-3,3λ+2,3-λ

We know the direction ratios of the line joining the points x1,y1,z1 and x2,y2,z2 is given by

x2-x1,y2-y1,z2-z1

Therefore, the direction ratios of line PM,

D.R of PM3λ-7,3λ-4,5-λ

Since, PM is perpendicular to the line

⇒  33λ-7+33λ-4-15-λ=0

⇒  λ=2

⇒  M3,8,1

Now distance of point P from point M,

⇒PM=3-42+8-62+1+22

⇒PM=14

The lines L1 and L2 given by

L1:x-12=y-31=z-22

Now any point on line L1 is given by,

P=2λ+1,λ+3,2λ+2

And any point on line L2 is given by,

Q=μ+2,2μ+2,3μ+3

Now equation of line L3 which passes through Q is given by,

 x-μ+11=y-2μ+2-1=z-3μ+3-2

Now it also passes through P, so we get,

⇒  2λ-μ-11=λ-2μ+1-1=2λ-3μ-1-2

Now on solving above equations we get, λ=μ=3

Hence, P7,6,8 and Q5,8,12

So, the distance PQ=26

solution:
Equation of plane through the line x-2y-z-5=0=x+y+3z-5 is

x-2y-z-5+λx+y+3z-5=0

⇒1+λx+-2+λ+-1+3λz+-5-5λ=0

Also, this plane is parallel to the line x+y+2z-7=0=2x+3y+z-2, so

1+λ-2+λ-1+3λ112231=0

⇒-51+λ+3λ-2+3λ-1=0

⇒-5-5λ+3λ-6+3λ-1=0

⇒λ=12

So, equation of plane is

13x+10y+35z=65   ...1

Hence, a=13, b=10, c=35

So, distance of the point a,b,c from plane 2x+2y-z+16=0 is

=26+20-35+164+4+1=9 units

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given, 

a→×b→×c→=b→-c→2

Using the vector triple cross product we have,

a→×b→×c→=a→·c→b→-a→·b→c→

So, (a→·c→)b→-(a→·b→)c→=b→-c→2

On comparing both sides we get,

a→·c→=12,a→·b→=12

∵ b→·d→ = a→·b→

∴b→·d→=12

Using vector cross-product formulae we have

(a→×b→)·(c→×d→)=a→·(b→×(c→×d→))

=a→·((b→·d→)c→-(b→·c→)d→)      ∵ b→·c→=0

=(a→·c→)(b→·d→)=12×12=14

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

The vector a→=-i^+2j^+k^ is rotated through a right angle, passing through the y-axis in its way and the resulting vector is b→

Now taking, b→=λa→×a→×j^ {note here we have taken cross vector with j^ as it is rotated along y-axis}

⇒b→=λ-i^+2j^+k^×-i^+2j^+k^×j^

⇒b→=λ-i^+2j^+k^×-i^-k^

⇒b→=λi^j^k^-121-10-1

⇒b→=λ-2i^-2j^+2k^

Also magnitude of both vector will be equal,

So,b→=a→⇒6=12 λ

⇒λ=±12

(Now λ=12 rejected ∵  b→ makes acute angle with y axis )

So, b→=-2-i^-j^+k^

Now projection of  3a→+2b→ on c→ is given by,

3a→+2b·c→c→=32

subject: Physics
solution:
Resonance frequency of an LC oscillations circuit is stated as 

ω0=1LC, here, L and C represents inductance and capacitance of circuit.

Now, when inductance and capacitance changes, i.e., L'=2L and C'=8C

Then, resonant frequency is ω=12L×8C=14LC

⇒ω=ω04

Thus, the value of x=14.

2000 Hz=12πL×62.5×10-9

L=14π2×20002×62.5×10-9=0.1 H=100 mH

subject: Physics
solution:
We have, 

Message frequency fm=5 kHz and carrier wave frequencyfc=2 MHz=2000 KHz

The resultant signal will have bandwidth of frequency given by

fc+fm-fc-fm

=[(2000+5)-(2000-5)] kHz

=10 kHz.

Thus, bandwidth for amplitude modulation is 10 kHz.

solution:
Given here, I=2 A, V=3.4 V, m=8.92×10-3 kg,

d=8.92×103 kg m-3 and ρ=1.7×10-8 Ω-m

From Ohm's law, resistance is R=VI=3.42=1.7 Ω

As resistance of wire is given by R=ρlA, where, A is area and l is length of wire.

 ⇒1.7=ρlA ⇒l=1.7Aρ

Now, volume=md=8.92×10-38.92×103=10-6 m3

Thus, 

l2=1.7Aρ×l=1.7volumelρ×l=1.7ρ10-6=1.71.7×10-8×10-6

Hence, l=10 m

subject: Physics
solution:
The de-Broglie wavelength of an electron accelerated through potential V is 

λ=hmv=h2 m(KE)=h2 meV

where, h is Planck's constant, e is charge of electron and m is mass of electron.

From above relation, λ∝1V

∴ λλ0=2040

⇒ λ=λ02.

subject: Physics
solution:
In an electromagnetic wave, the direction of energy flow is along the direction of the wave propagation. Therefore, the wave propagates along negative z direction. 

Electric field is along the positive y direction and the wave propagates along negative z direction. Thus, magnetic field is in direction perpendicular to both z and y axes. 

Using vector algebra, 

(-k^)=(+j^)×i^

Hence, magnetic field is in positive x direction.

subject: Physics
solution:
Given here, kinetic energy 12e=12mux2⇒ux=em

Acceleration of electron due to electric field is a=eEm, where, e is charge on electron.

Using equation of motion, s=uxt+12axt2  along x direction, we get

L=uxt+0∵ax=0⇒10×10-2=uxt⇒t=10-1ux    ....1

Using equation of motion, vy=uy+ayt along y direction, we get

vy=0+eEmt=eEmt   ...2

From equation 1 and 2, we have

vy=eEm10-1ux

Now, tanθ=vyvx=eEm10-1ux2=e×10×10-1m×em=1

The angle of deviation of the path of electron as it comes out of the field is θ=45°.

subject: Physics
solution:
The gravitational force acting on a particle of mass m at a distance r (<R) from the centre of Earth is

F=-GM'mr2  ....(1)

where M' is the mass of portion of Earth enclosed by a sphere of radius r concentric with Earth. Here - sign indicates radially inward direction of force.

Taking the density of Earth to be uniform

M'4πr3=M4πR3

⇒M'=Mr3R3   ....(2)

Substituting M' from equation 2 into equation 1:

F=-GMmR3 r

Which is similar to the equation of motion F=-kx with k=GMmR3=mgR (∵g=GMR2)

For such motion, the time period is given by:

T=2πmk

Substituting k from above,

T=2πRg

Putting the values, we get

T=2×3.146400×10310=2×3.14×800≈1 hour 24 minutes

subject: Physics
solution:
Time period of a simple pendulum at Earth's surface is given by

T=2πlg.

When distance from centre of Earth, i.e., at h=R, acceleration due to gravity is g'=g1+hR2=g22=g4

Now, time period is  xT=2πlg4

⇒xT=2×2πlg

⇒xT=2T⇒x=2

Thus, x=2.

subject: Physics
options: Inversely proportional to square root of temperature (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>T</mi></mfrac></msqrt></math>).
Proportional to square root of temperature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>T</mi></msqrt></math>.
solution:
Root mean speed of a gas molecule is stated as vrms=3RTM, where, R is the universal gas constant, T is temperature, and M is the molar mass of the gas.

Thus, vrms∝T.

subject: Physics
solution:
(A)

Here net magnetic field will be sum of magnetic field due to straight wires ab, de and due to loop bcd.

So, Bab=μ04πIr=Bde (directed out of the plane in both cases)

Bbcd=μ04πIr(2π) (in the plane)

Thus, magnetic field at O is

BO=-μ04πIr+μ04πIr(2π)-μ04πIr

BO=μ02πIr(π-1)  (III)

(B)

Here net magnetic field will be sum of magnetic field due to straight wires ab, de and semicircular arc bcd.

Bab=μ04πIr=Bde (directed out of the plane in both cases)

Bbcd=μ04πIr(π) (out of the plane)

Thus, net magnetic field at O is

BO=μ04πIr+μ04πIr(π)+μ04πIr

B0=μ04πIr(π+2) (I)

(C)

Here net magnetic field will be sum of magnetic field due to straight wires ab, de and due to loop bcd.

Bab=μ04πIr (directed in the plane)

Bbcd=μ04πIr(π) (in the plane)

Bde=0 (passing through the axis)

Thus, magnetic field at O is

BO=μ04πIr(π+1)  (IV)

(D)

Here net magnetic field will be sum of magnetic field due to straight wires ab, de and due to loop bcd.

Bab=0=Bde (passing through the axis)

Bbcd=μ04πIr(π) (out of the plane)

Thus, magnetic field at O is

BO=μ0I4r (II).

A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II

subject: Physics
solution:
Given, 

Number of turns per metre n=12002

 l= 2 m, d=0.2 m and I=2 A

Magnetic field at centre inside the solenoid is given by B=μ0nI, where, μ0 is the permeability constant.

Magnetic intensity is related to magnetic field by 

H=Bμ0=μ0nIμ0=nI=12002(2)

⇒H=1.2×103 A m-1
